div.p: Your telegram 8th instant received.See Foreign Relations, 1894, Appendix II, p. 1397. Sentences of death not yet known to have been approved by President. Sentences in 25 cases approved and made public, viz: Twenty-three Hawaiians, five to ten years’ hard labor; one Englishman, Col. V. V. Ashford, one year and fine) and one American, J. F. Bowler, five years and fine of $5,000. These two charged with misprision of treason. All required to don prison garb. One hundred and twenty tried, 21 more now on joint trial, 2 acquitted, 3 deported, and 15 of different nationalities released on condition of leaving country. Over 200 yet in prison, of whom 13 are American $ latter confined without formal charges or trial. Feeling less extreme.
